creative_brief: Founder Rachel in a real grocery aisle looking frustrated (before), then smiling at a FarmThru hub holding labelled farm produce (after). Split-screen or sequence format. Warm, natural lighting. No text overlay except the FarmThru logo.
primary_text:
"You can't actually know where grocery store food comes from."

I heard that for years. And honestly, I believed it too.

Before FarmThru, I'd stand in the aisle reading labels that told me nothing. 'Product of Australia' on pork that travelled through three wholesalers. 'Farm fresh' eggs from places with no farm name attached.

After: I built a grocery store with zero warehouses, zero wholesalers, zero middlemen. Bundarra Farm pork. Farmer Brown eggs. Paris Creek bio-dynamic dairy. Every product traceable to the people who grew it.

No commitment. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

We're growing, and you can own a piece of it.
headline: I built the grocery store I needed
description: Real farms named on every product. Fair prices. Be part of what comes next.
